title: Marriage (Nikah) Registration & Computerized MRC Guide
legal_basis: Muslim Family Laws Ordinance 1961 (Section 5) + Provincial Rules

whats_this.heading: Nikah Nama & MRC Wizard
whats_this.description: All Muslim marriages in Pakistan must be registered at the Union Council (or equivalent). After UC registration, a computerized Marriage Registration Certificate (MRC) is issued. This wizard helps you navigate the registration steps, documents, and attestation requirements.
quick_instructions: Select your specific marriage case case (Standard, Foreign Partner, etc.)
Review the estimated fee structure for 2026
Follow the step-by-step registration and attestation roadmap
whats_this.disclaimer: Fees and procedures are subject to change by local Union Councils. Always confirm with your local UC Secretary.

special_note.title: Attestation Note
special_note.description: Costs for MOFA attestation or certified translations are separate from registration fees.

document_need.title: Marriage Case Details
document_need.description: Help us customize your guide by selecting the scenario that fits your marriage.

options.title: Foreign Partner
options.description: One partner is a foreign national (e.g. US/UK Citizen)

options.title: Second Marriage
options.description: One or both partners are Divorced or Widowed

options.title: Age-Specific Case
options.description: Bride is under the age of 18

onsitePhases.title: Nikah Ceremony
onsitePhases.description: Licensed Nikah Khawan fills the handwritten Urdu Nikah Nama.

onsitePhases.title: UC Registration
onsitePhases.description: Submit Urdu Nikah Nama and CNICs to Union Council for registration.

onsitePhases.title: NADRA Issuance
onsitePhases.description: Collect the computerized Marriage Registration Certificate (MRC).

onsitePhases.title: MOFA Attestation
onsitePhases.description: Get the certificate attested by Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Mandatory for Visas).

documents_checklist.label: Original Urdu Nikah Nama
documents_checklist.description: Signed by Nikah Khawan, Bride, Groom, and Witnesses

documents_checklist.label: CNIC of Both Fathers
documents_checklist.description: Copies of bride's father and groom's father CNICs

documents_checklist.label: Passport Photos
documents_checklist.description: Two passport-size photos with white background

documents_checklist.label: Foreign Passport Copy
documents_checklist.description: Including entry stamp page to Pakistan

documents_checklist.label: Affidavit of Free Will
documents_checklist.description: Usually required when one spouse is a foreigner

documents_checklist.label: Valid Visa / POC / NICOP
documents_checklist.description: For foreign nationals or overseas Pakistanis

documents_checklist.label: Divorce or Death Certificate
documents_checklist.description: NADRA Divorce Certificate or Spouse's Death Certificate

documents_checklist.label: B-Form / Birth Certificate
documents_checklist.description: Proof of age for younger applicants

validation.title: Certificate Validation
validation.description: Double check all computerized details before finalizing your paperwork.

upload_section.title: Upload Certificate
upload_section.description: Store your computerized MRC securely in your vault.

items.label: Spelling matches CNIC and Passport exactly
Father's and Husband's names are correctly listed
CNIC numbers are accurate without typos
Official seal and QR code are clear
Registration date is within 30 days of Nikah

tips: Focus on the Computerized Certificate: The handwritten Urdu Nikah Nama alone is often not accepted by embassies.
Ensure names on the Nikah Nama match the CNIC and Passport exactly to avoid attestation delays.
Always use a licensed Nikah Khawan registered with the relevant Union Council.
pitfalls: Incorrect Spelling: Even a one-letter difference can cause rejection during MOFA attestation.
Delayed Registration: Failing to register within 30 days requires a complex affidavit for late entry.

tips: MOFA Attestation: The certificate must be attested by MOFA for any international visa application.
Certified Translation: If your UC only issues a partial translation, use a certified service for a full English copy.
Keep multiple certified copies (at least 5-6) of the computerized certificate for future use.
pitfalls: Column 21 Neglect: Leaving column 21 (regarding special conditions) blank can be a major hurdle later.
Missing Father's Name: Ensure the certificates reflect the father's name correctly as per identity documents.

tips: Verify the database: Some embassies check the NADRA database directly; ensure your certificate is properly synced.
In case of a married female, her CNIC should be updated with husband's name for certain visa types.
pitfalls: Errors in MRC digits: Typos in CNIC digits on the MRC will prevent future software updates (like FRC).
